## prompt-final-narrative

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-narrative.md`
(SUB-PROMPT SCHEDULE item 4), adapted from
`single-prompt-bill/auto-bill-02/sub-prompts/prompt-7-final-bill.md` and the
final-paper polish of
`cancer-automated/tree/main/papers/VVUQ-02/final-paper`.*

Write the final-narrative into `law-physical-ai-trials/review/final-narrative/`
by carrying the full-narrative to publication quality. Read the full-narrative
critically, list the corrections it needs, and implement them here: this is the
self-learning step in which the lessons identified from the full stage are
applied. The final manuscript is the submission-ready version a senior author
would sign.

**Corrections to identify and apply (senior-author finishing).**

- White space: remove large empty bands; where interword spacing looks uneven,
  tune `\RaggedRight`, `\emergencystretch`, and `\tolerance` so words are evenly
  spaced with no right-margin overflow.
- Pagination: no orphan or widow line, no one or two word last line, no single
  line stranded onto the next page; balance each page with `\clearpage`,
  `\vspace`, and `\hspace` so each page stands on its own without overcrowding.
- Tables: every table matches the body measure exactly at `\textwidth`, every
  column is `>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}` (left aligned, ragged
  right, no large inter-word gaps), referenced with `\autoref`.
- Figures: the colored TikZ Mermaid figures are refined from the full stage
  (cleaner routing, consistent palette, captions that explain rather than
  narrate), kept strictly to black, grayscales, `#EBCB8B`, `#D08770`, `#8B2E3F`.
- Symbols: every section reference uses "§", never "SS"; single hyphens only (no
  en, em, double, or triple dashes); straight quotes consistent with the
  template.
- Prose: definitions precede first use; ideas connect across sections; each
  emotional pillar closes by pointing the legislator at the specific provision
  of H. R. 9510 that answers it; the recurring questions resolve into a clear
  call to action.

**Sources.** Same evidence base as the full stage
(`review/references/author_works.bib`, `review/references/narrative_refs.bib`,
`cancer-automated/papers/VVUQ-02/`, the Bill DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20619762), now
cited cleanly with the `ieeetr` style and `\nocite{*}` so every entry renders and
each DOI prints its clickable resolver URL.

One commit each for `main.tex`, the `.sty`, `references.bib`, and `README.md`;
one commit per `sections/*.tex` file; plus `prompt-final-narrative.md` and
`output-final-narrative.md`. For the second-to-last commit fix all errors across
all files; for the last commit write the directory README and the
`final-narrative-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle and refresh the pull request. The
manuscript must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX and preserve the template
visual theme. After this stage, the single v0.1.0 release update follows (root
`CHANGELOG.md`, `releases.md`, and the main `README.md` with its added version
section, badges, ASCII and colored Mermaid diagrams, and repository structure).
Commit and push in real time; do not hold commits. Do not stall, ask questions,
or go into plan mode.
